title: Job Description Decomposition and Evidence Mapping · Opportunity Brief: Go No Go
summary: A listing combines recruiter language, team outcomes, essential work, preferences, copied requirements, benefits, legal text, and ambiguous signals. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go no go increment in role requirement and evidence matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.
Separate observable opportunity evidence from speculative culture, prestige, tone, badge, and keyword interpretations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Go No Go · Theory Go No Go
steps.instruction:
theory case — A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Go No Go contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Go No Go · Theory Go No Go.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities. For Predict Go No Go · Theory Go No Go, keep Go No Go connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Go No Go condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: O*NET Employer-Based In-Demand Software Skills — Supports time- and location-bounded technology demand comparisons while prohibiting claims that posting frequency equals job importance or learner fit.

hints: For Predict Go No Go · Theory Go No Go, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Go No Go precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Predict Go No Go · Theory Go No Go, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

For Predict Go No Go · Theory Go No Go, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Applying has no cost, so every listing deserves a submission.

options.text: For predict go no go, apply Go No Go without its stated preconditions, omit the role requirement and evidence matrix trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Go No Go for role requirement and evidence matrix.
Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Applying has no cost, so every listing deserves a submission. That claim fails the predict go no go evidence boundary for Go No Go.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities. For Bound Go No Go · Theory Go No Go, keep Go No Go connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Go No Go while producing role requirement and evidence matrix.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The worked example variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities. A defensible solution applies Go No Go, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to role requirement and evidence matrix.

content.code:
# Worked focus: career-go-no-go — Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-4
focus: career-go-no-go
decision-evidence: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Applying has no cost, so every listing deserves a submission. This fails the Go No Go contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: O*NET Employer-Based In-Demand Software Skills (U.S. postings from 2025; reviewed 2026-07-15) — Supports time- and location-bounded technology demand comparisons while prohibiting claims that posting frequency equals job importance or learner fit.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.
